landing.title: Know who is leaving — before they do.
landing.subtitle: Soral scores each worker for turnover risk, explains why, and hands supervisors the play to retain them — a week before they walk.
landing.featuresTitle: Everything you need to act on attrition
landing.featuresSubtitle: From signal to intervention, in one workspace.
landing.f1Desc: Every worker as a dot, ranked by 30-day turnover risk. Hot spots stand out at a glance.
landing.f2Desc: SHAP contributions show why the model flags each person — no black box.
landing.f3Desc: A retention play generated for the manager, ready to assign to the supervisor.
landing.f4Desc: RBAC out of the box and a fully multilingual UI — English, Spanish and Italian.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Turn turnover from a surprise into a plan.
landing.positioning: Built for maquiladoras on the US–Mexico border.
landing.howSubtitle: From raw HR data to a retention play — in three steps.
landing.step1Desc: Import headcount, attendance and HR signals via CSV, SFTP or API.
landing.step2Desc: The model ranks turnover risk per worker and shows the why with SHAP.
landing.step3Desc: Supervisors get a recommendation and assign it to act this week.
landing.explainTitle: No black box. Every score, explained.
landing.explainText: Soral shows the SHAP contribution of each factor and a six-dimension risk profile, so a supervisor knows exactly why a worker is flagged — and what to act on.

model.title: Model & transparency
model.subtitle: How the turnover score is built, how accurate it is, and the guardrails around it.
model.calibSub: Predicted vs. observed turnover — the closer to the diagonal, the better calibrated.
model.howBody: Each worker gets a 0–100 probability of leaving in the next 30 days, combined from six dimensions of signal. SHAP shows exactly which factors drive each score — there is no black box.
model.confBody: Confidence is lower near the 80% decision boundary and higher at the extremes. Treat a low-confidence score as a prompt to look closer — not to act blindly.
model.gov1: No adverse action: a score must never justify discipline or termination — only retention support.
model.gov2: No protected attributes are used as features; bias is monitored across shifts, lines and tenure.
model.gov3: Every score is explainable (SHAP) and every assigned action is audit-logged.
model.gov4: Human in the loop: supervisors decide and act — the model only prioritizes.
